user_request: Run the cogworks-learn skill to review my skill structure.
Use /cogworks-learn and explain how you'd approach this before running any commands.
notes: Explicit invocation should activate without premature execution commands

user_request: How should I structure a skill so it triggers correctly across agents?
notes: Implicit cross-agent skill-authoring guidance should activate

user_request: I am writing a new skill and need help with frontmatter and invocation modes.

notes: General coding help should not activate

user_request: Analyse and synthesize these three API documentation pages into a reference document.
notes: Multi-source synthesis is cogworks-encode domain, not cogworks-learn authoring guidance

user_request: Generate a skill for analyzing code quality across multiple TypeScript files in a repo.
expected_content: Make all independent tool calls in parallel
notes: Skills with multiple independent operations (reading files) should include parallel tool use instruction

user_request: Generate a skill for running test suites and summarizing failures.
expected_content: Delegate this task to a subagent
notes: High-volume result tasks (test runs) should include subagent delegation guidance

user_request: Generate a Claude Code skill for batch log analysis.
notes: [Claude Code only] context: fork should be labeled as CC-specific when mentioned for batch log analysis

user_request: Generate a skill for enforcing code style rules that should apply to every session.
notes: Rules for every session should recommend persistent config (CLAUDE.md, copilot-instructions.md) not a skill

user_request: Generate a skill that takes a file path as an argument using $ARGUMENTS.
notes: $ARGUMENTS should be labeled as Claude Code-specific, not universal

user_request: Generate a skill using disable-model-invocation and $ARGUMENTS for a deployment workflow.
notes: Skills using Claude Code-specific features should include compatibility field in frontmatter

user_request: Generate a skill with restricted tool access using allowed-tools.
notes: allowed-tools should be described as broadly supported (16/18 agents), not experimental or Claude-specific

user_request: Generate a skill that blocks any git push without running tests first.
notes: Deterministic enforcement (block git push) should recommend a hook, not a skill — hooks use exit code 2 to block actions with zero AI discretion

user_request: Generate a skill for a specialized code review agent with restricted tools and a custom model.
notes: Custom agent with restricted tools and model is a subagent definition, not a skill — should recommend subagent definition instead

user_request: Generate a Claude Code skill for batch log analysis that delegates to the right subagent type.
notes: Batch log analysis is read-only — should select Explore subagent type (Haiku, Read/Grep/Glob) and label context:fork as CC-specific

user_request: Generate a Claude Code skill that runs three independent research tasks in parallel using subagents.
notes: Independent research tasks should use background dispatch for concurrent execution, not sequential foreground calls
